blocks.text: Rear differential disassembly and assembly
1. development

items: If the rear differential has been disassembled or reassembled after installation in the vehicle Viscous couplings are inspected and again Check the amount of rear differential fluid.

blocks.text: 1. Rear differential housing removal
Loosen and remove the bolts (A) diagonally, and remove the rear differential housing (B). Remove the two rear differential oil seals (C).
2. Remove rear differential case assembly.
Remove the breather plate (B) from the rear differential carrier (A). Remove the rear differential case assembly (C). Remove 61mm shim (D).

items: Check the mounting position before removing the 61 mm shims and install them in the same position when mounting.

blocks.text: 3. Viscous coupling assembly removal
Caulk and loosen the 18mm locknut. The 18mm locknut is left-hand threaded.

items: Caulk the 18mm lock nut sufficiently and be careful not to damage the shaft.

blocks.text: Remove the 18mm locknut (A). Remove the spring washer (B). Remove the washer (C). Remove the backup ring (D). Remove the O-ring (E). Remove the viscous coupling assembly (F).
4. Drive Pinion Gear, Rear Differential Spacer Removal
Remove the drive pinion gear (B) from the rear differential carrier (A). Remove the rear differential spacer (C) from the drive pinion gear.
5. Remove oil seal
Remove the oil seal (B) from the rear differential carrier (A). Remove the taper roller bearing (C).
6. Remove drain bolt and filler bolt
Remove the drain bolt (A) and sealing washer (B). Remove the filler bolt (C) and sealing washer (D).

items: The rear differential shall be assembled while checking and adjusting the starting torque of the bearings.
Hypoid gear oil shall be applied to each part during assembly.
If the rear differential has been disassembled or reassembled after installation in the vehicle Viscous couplings are inspected and again Check the amount of rear differential fluid.

blocks.text: 1. Drain bolt, filler bolt mounting
Install a new sealing washer (A) and drain bolt (B). Install a new sealing washer (C) and filler bolt (D).
2. Drive Pinion Gear, Rear Differential Spacer Installation
Install new rear differential spacer (A) on drive pinion gear (B). Install the drive pinion gear to the rear differential carrier (C). Install taper roller bearing (D).
3. Oil Seal Installation
Install a new oil seal (A) using the dedicated tool.
4. Viscous coupling assembly. installation
Install viscous coupling assembly (A). Install a new O-ring (B). Install a new backup ring (C). Install washer (D). Install spring washer (E). Install a new 18 mm locknut (F).

items: Pay attention to the assembly direction of the spring washers.

blocks.text: Tighten the 18mm lock nut to the lower torque limit. The 18mm locknut is left-hand threaded.
5. Preload Adjustment
Rotate the taper roller bearings several times to acclimate them and measure the preload.

items.text: Gradually tighten the 18mm locknut to enter the standard preload.
Gradually tighten the 18mm locknut to enter the standard preload.
If the pre-load exceeds 1.51 N-m (15.4 kgf-cm), the rear differential spacer must be replaced. 18 mm lock nuts must not be loosened for adjustment.
If the pre-load exceeds 1.51 N-m (15.4 kgf-cm), the rear differential spacer must be replaced. 18 mm lock nuts must not be loosened for adjustment.
If the standard preload is not reached even after the tightening torque exceeds 177 N-m (18.0 kgf-m), the rear differential spacer shall be replaced and adjusted again.
If the standard preload is not reached even after the tightening torque exceeds 177 N-m (18.0 kgf-m), the rear differential spacer shall be replaced and adjusted again.

blocks.text: Caulk the 18mm lock nut securely into the recess on the shaft. Caulk to a depth of at least 0.7 mm using a punch with a tip diameter of 3.0-3.5 mm. Ensure that there are no cracks or other damage to the caulking area.
6. Rear differential case assembly.
Install the rear differential case assembly (A) onto the rear differential carrier (B). Install two 61mm shims (C). Install the breather plate (D).

items: When installing 61mm shims, install them in the same position as when disassembled.

blocks.text: 7. Rear differential housing installation
Apply sealant to the sealing surfaces of the rear differential housing and rear differential carrier.

items: The sealant must be genuine Honda liquid packing 1216E.
Wipe off old sealant and remove dirt and oil from the sealing surface.
If the sealant is left for more than 4 minutes after application, do not assemble the product as it is. Re-apply the sealant.
Oil should be injected 30 minutes after assembly.

blocks.text: Attach the rear differential housing (A) to the rear differential carrier (B) and tighten the six bolts (C) diagonally.
8. Left Rear Differential Oil Seal Installation

Apply Honda Genuine Fluid Packing 1216E to the mating surface contact areas (2 locations) of the new oil seal (A) between the rear differential housing and carrier. Press-fit the oil seal using the dedicated tool.

items: Wipe off old sealant and remove dirt and oil from the sealing surface.
If the sealant is left for more than 4 minutes after application, do not assemble the product as it is. Re-apply the sealant.
Oil should be injected 30 minutes after assembly.
Press-fit the oil seal to the end face (B).

blocks.text: 9. Right Side Rear Differential Oil Seal Installation

Apply Honda Genuine Fluid Packing 1216E to the mating surface contact areas (2 locations) of the new oil seal (A) between the rear differential housing and carrier. Press-fit the oil seal using the dedicated tool.

items: Wipe off old sealant and remove dirt and oil from the sealing surface.
If the sealant is left for more than 4 minutes after application, do not assemble the product as it is. Re-apply the sealant.
Oil should be injected 30 minutes after assembly.
The oil seal is located from the end face (B) to the 8.6－9.6mm Press-fit at the position of
